news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad civic body seals nine RMC plants for violation of environmental norms
news.para: Nine ready-mix concrete plants in Pimpri Chinchwad have been sealed for violating environmental norms, including inadequate dust control and open mixing. This action follows a crackdown by the municipal corporation and the Maharashtra Pollution Control Board due to rising air pollution. Residents had complained about dust and debris from these plants, contributing to a significant drop in air quality.

news.title: Odisha HC allows vigilance court trial in BDA plot allotment scam case
news.para: The Orissa High Court has refused to discharge former Bhubaneswar Development Authority officials accused in a 2014 plot allotment scam. The court found sufficient material for the trial to proceed. The vigilance probe alleges irregular allotment of commercial plots to relatives of officials without advertisement and at low prices.

news.title: Greater Noida authority launches ₹3,600-crore commercial plot scheme
news.para: Greater Noida Industrial Development Authority has launched a major commercial land scheme, offering 37 plots with a combined reserve price of Rs 3,604 crore. These plots, designated for commercial complexes, retail hubs, and mixed-use developments, are available through e-auction. Registrations are open until December 5, with plot sizes and prices varying across several key sectors.

news.title: Allahabad HC junks Noida authority’s extension charge demand against Windsor corporate tower
news.para: Allahabad High Court quashed a Rs 12 crore demand by Noida Authority against Windsor Corporate Tower. The court ruled extension charges must be based on the original lease premium, not current rates. Noida was directed to recalculate charges and refund any excess amount with interest.

news.title: Nagpur civic body seeks freehold status for 332 plots in Shiv Nagar
news.para: The Nagpur Municipal Corporation is proposing to grant freehold status to 332 plots in Shiv Nagar, a move strongly backed by local MLA and the Chief Minister. This decision has sparked accusations of favoritism from plot holders in other city layouts, who are now demanding similar relief ahead of civic elections.

news.title: Mumbai: BMC housing lottery draws 2,037 applicants for 426 homes
news.para: The BMC's lottery for 426 affordable homes saw a modest response with 2,037 applications, significantly lower than MHADA's draws. The flats, priced between Rs 55 lakh and Rs 1 crore, are for EWS and LIG categories. This marks BMC's first independent lottery to streamline the process and avoid processing fees.

news.title: Bar Council of Punjab and Haryana clears M3M India's directors
news.para: The Bar Council of Punjab & Haryana's privilege committee has exonerated M3M India directors Roop Bansal and Yateesh Wahal, along with 17 lawyers, from bench hunting and forum shopping accusations. An inquiry found no evidence of malicious intent or case influence, deeming withdrawals and re-filings routine. M3M India hailed the decision as a validation of procedural integrity.

news.title: Bengaluru development body engineers push for appeal against RERA’s ‘promoter’ order
news.para: BDA engineers are pushing for an appeal against K-Rera's order classifying the agency as a promoter. This classification subjects BDA projects, like NPKL, to Rera's strict timelines and regulations, which officials find challenging. A board meeting in December will decide on the appeal, following legal advice.

news.title: Kolkata: KMRC razes two more buildings for reconstruction of cave-in zone houses
news.para: Two more houses were demolished in Kolkata's Bowbazar metro collapse zone to facilitate the reconstruction of homes damaged in the 2019 tunnelling disaster. This move clears steel beams obstructing new construction, with 23 houses planned for Durga Pituri Lane. While many residents have returned, 72 families remain displaced, awaiting their rebuilt homes.

news.title: RWAs in Gurugram get six weeks to fix defunct rainwater harvesting systems
news.para: Gurugram authorities have directed developers and RWAs to restore inactive rainwater harvesting systems in high-rise societies within six weeks, with proof of functionality due by December 31. A recent inspection revealed nearly 90% of these systems were non-operational. The GMDA emphasized the critical role of these systems in mitigating waterlogging and addressing water scarcity in the rapidly developing city.

news.title: Irregularities in Haryana’s Wazirabad tehsil ahead of online property registry launch, probe on
news.para: An inquiry has been ordered into alleged large-scale irregularities in Wazirabad tehsil's property registrations just before the system went online. Reports suggest manual registrations of deeds linked to builders with suspended licenses and outstanding dues, including Ansal Buildwell and Ireo, occurred despite official restrictions. This follows similar investigations in other tehsils uncovering illegal registries and revenue losses.

news.title: Mumbai: JVPD bungalows giving way to buildings as families grow & FSI up four times
news.para: Once a tranquil enclave for Bollywood stars, JVPD is now undergoing rapid redevelopment, transforming from low-rise bungalows to luxury high-rises. This shift, driven by rising property prices and changing family needs, has led to increased density, reduced open spaces, and strained infrastructure, raising concerns about the city's unsustainable future.

news.title: RERA helps enhance transparency in property deals: Tamil Nadu RERA chairman
news.para: Tamil Nadu RERA Chairman Shiv Das Meena highlighted how the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act (RERA) empowers homebuyers to verify projects online, fostering transparency and accountability. The Act ensures a safe investment environment and offers a structured grievance redressal system for both buyers and developers.

news.title: Collectors in Goa told to upload land conversion sanads online
news.para: Goa State Information Commission has ordered district collectors to put land conversion sanad details online. This follows a complaint about non-compliance with the Right to Information Act. Applicants' names, addresses, survey numbers, and conversion dates will be uploaded. The directive applies to both South and North Goa districts.

news.title: Smart city to conduct full study on Panaji revitalisation
news.para: Imagine Panaji Smart City Development Limited will conduct a comprehensive study to assess the condition of core business zone and heritage buildings. The project, funded by a special assistance scheme, will analyze roads, drains, and waterworks, and identify restoration needs for heritage structures, particularly in Fontainhas. The study aims to preserve Portuguese-era architecture and modernize city infrastructure.

news.title: Bhubaneswar: Department upholds demolition of unauthorised five-storey residential building in Balianta
news.para: The housing department upheld a demolition order for an unauthorised five-storey building in Andilo, Balianta. A promoter and homebuyers' appeal was dismissed after they attempted to bypass BDA approval, relying on an old gram panchayat plan. The structure, built without NOCs, will be demolished with costs recoverable from the promoter and landowners.

news.title: Bombay HC defers builder's pre-arrest bail plea hearing to November 27
news.para: The Bombay High Court has deferred the hearing on builder Bhavesh Sanghrajka's pre-arrest bail plea to November 27. Sanghrajka is seeking protection in connection with an FIR for alleged culpable homicide after a woman was killed by a falling cement block from his construction site. The court seeks an expert report on safety measures taken.

news.title: Nagpur consumer forum sentences builder to two-year jail, fines for defying  order
news.para: A city builder in Nagpur has been sentenced to two years imprisonment and fined ₹10,000 for wilfully disobeying a 2016 consumer court order. The builder deliberately delayed compensation for over nine years, only paying the principal amount without interest or damages. The commission emphasized that national legal obligations cannot be ignored for financial convenience.

news.title: LuLu Group revives mall project in Visakhapatnam
news.para: LuLu Group International will revive its Visakhapatnam shopping mall project after a hiatus, citing circumstances beyond their control during the previous government's tenure. Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu's invitation prompted the revival, with construction set to begin this week and completion expected within three years, creating thousands of jobs.

news.title: New online system to simplify building plan approvals in Uttar Pradesh
news.para: Uttar Pradesh government is set to launch FASTPAS, a new online system for building plan approvals. This advanced platform will simplify the process and is expected to reduce fees. Trials have been successful, and the rollout is anticipated within a month. FASTPAS will replace the current OBPAS system.
